title: Ohm's Law
content: The current through a conductor is directly proportional to the voltage across it and inversely proportional to its resistance: V = I · R.
extended_content: Ohm's Law applies to linear, resistive, and time-invariant elements at constant temperature.  It forms the basis for DC circuit analysis and is used alongside Kirchhoff's laws for complex networks.
units: V: Volts, I: Amperes, R: Ohms (Ω)
source: Hayt & Kemmerly, Engineering Circuit Analysis, 8th ed.
